summary: Patrick McKenzie announces the name of his new LLC, Kalzumeus Software, ending months of referring to his upcoming venture as 'the second project.' He explains his philosophy that product names matter far more than company names for micro-ISVs, since the company is just a legal fiction for interfacing with governments and corporations. The name comes from a talking dragon in a long-running RPG campaign, chosen partly because it was easy to secure as a domain name due to its uniqueness. He outlines plans to transfer Bingo Card Creator to the new LLC and launch a second product under the Kalzumeus banner.
key_points: Product names matter more than company names for micro-ISVs because customers identify with products, not corporate entities
A company is fundamentally a legal fiction for relating to governments and corporations who expect entity-to-entity relationships
Unique, hard-to-spell names have the advantage of easy domain acquisition and zero Google competition
McKenzie plans to transfer Bingo Card Creator to the new LLC and launch a second product simultaneously
money_quotes: I am an ardent proponent of the theory that product names are more important for branding purposes than company names as a uISV.

After all, the company is just a legal fiction designed to give me an easier way to relate government authorities and other corporations who expect entity-to-entity rather than person-to-person relationships.
Almost all of the folks who I'd really want to be interested in my ongoing concern rather than my products identify it by my name.
Its 9 letters long, easy to mispell, and means nothing to most people.
Unless this post has been indexed by the time you read this, its totally unique on Google.
As the dragon was smart, cheeky, and just a little insane I thought that was as good a mascot for a software company as anything.
key_insight: For a micro-ISV, the company name is just legal plumbing — what matters for branding is the product name, since that's what customers actually encounter and remember.
